title: Strawberry custard tart
subtitle: Sweet pastry shell with vanilla custard and fresh strawberries
excerpt: A classic French-style custard tart: blind-baked sweet pastry shell, set vanilla custard, and a thick layer of halved fresh strawberries arranged on top. The custard is firm enough to slice cleanly. Made in summer when strawberries need no improvement.
sourceNotes: Fruit custard tarts in the French patisserie tradition appear throughout European baking history. The format of a blind-baked sweet pastry shell (pate sucree), a set creme patissiere, and fresh fruit on top is a standard patisserie preparation found in bakeries across France and now widely produced across the UK. The recipe follows the British home-baking adaptation of the French tarte aux fraises tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry shell can be blind-baked the day before. The custard can be made a day ahead and refrigerated, covered with cling film pressed to the surface. Assemble and top with strawberries no more than 3 hours before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven for blind baking the shell; the custard is cooked on the hob, not in the oven

glossaryTerms.definition: Creme patissiere (pastry cream) is thickened with both egg yolks and cornflour, which makes it stiffer and more stable than a pouring custard. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with a whisk, until the mixture boils and thickens. It must reach a full boil to cook the cornflour. The finished cream should be very thick and glossy. Press cling film directly onto the surface while it cools to prevent a skin forming.

content.text: This tart is assembled in three separate stages, each of which can be done ahead. The
must reach a full boil to thicken properly. Let it cool completely before filling the shell, otherwise the pastry will soften.

Rub 100 g cold butter into 200 g flour and 40 g icing sugar. Add 1 egg yolk and 1 to 2 tbsp cold water to form a firm pastry. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 190 degrees C fan. Roll to 3 mm and line a greased 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Chill for 15 minutes. Line with baking paper and fill with baking weights or dried beans. Blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove paper and weights and bake for a further 10 to 12 minutes until golden and dry throughout. Cool completely.

Whisk 3 egg yolks with 70 g caster sugar, 25 g cornflour, and 1 tsp vanilla until smooth. Heat 300 ml whole milk until just below a boil. Pour a third of the hot milk into the egg mixture, whisking constantly, then pour everything back into the pan. Cook over medium heat, whisking continuously, until the mixture thickens and boils, about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from heat. Add 20 g butter and whisk until smooth. Press cling film directly onto the surface and refrigerate until cold, at least 1 hour.
Beat the cold creme patissiere briefly to loosen. Spread evenly into the cold pastry shell. Arrange 400 g halved strawberries over the top, cut-side down. Serve within 3 hours.

items.solution: The heat was too high or the stirring was not continuous. Lumps can usually be removed by beating vigorously off the heat, or pressing through a sieve while still hot.

items.problem: Pastry is soggy at the base
items.solution: The shell was not fully baked, or the creme patissiere was not cold enough when spread in. The shell should be completely crisp and golden throughout. The cream must be fully cold before filling.
